flashcards.back: A community of living organisms (biotic) and their non-living physical environment (abiotic), interacting as a system.
Only about 10% of the energy is transferred from one trophic level to the next in a food chain; the rest is lost as heat through metabolic processes.

flashcards.front: What is the key difference between a food chain and a food web?
flashcards.back: A food chain is a single, linear path of energy flow. A food web is a complex network of interconnected food chains, showing all possible feeding relationships.

flashcards.front: Name the four types of ecosystem services.
Give an example of a detritivore.
flashcards.back: Earthworms, millipedes, or woodlice. They consume dead organic matter (detritus).
